title: marginaria loricea
intro: Marginaria loricea, also known as the sweet-scented margin, is a species of flowering plant in the family Crassulaceae. It is a succulent plant that has been traditionally used for its medicinal properties, particularly in treating respiratory issues such as bronchitis and coughs, due to its expectorant and anti-inflammatory properties. The health benefits of Marginaria loricea include its ability to reduce fever, alleviate congestion, and soothe sore throats, making it a valuable natural remedy in folk medicine. The plant can be prepared as a tea or tincture, and its leaves can be dried and used to make infusions or added to ointments for topical application.
